reasoning: The starting value on 2025-11-23 is anchored around 6.044 (the Nov 21 reading). This value represents a sharp one-day drop from 6.129 on Nov 20. Over the past month, the FHA 30-year index has consistently averaged closer to 6.07-6.08, indicating that 6.044 is near the lower boundary of the recent localized range. According to the FRED statistical tool, there is roughly a 64% chance that the index will exceed 6.044 by Nov 30, largely due to basic mean-reversion expectations and seasonal upward drift seen around late November in previous years. News sentiment suggests that 10-year Treasury yields are expected to remain flat or rise slightly over the next few quarters toward 4.21%, supporting higher mortgage rates rather than rapid declines. Consequently, the probability of exceeding 6.044 is highest in the short term (Nov 30) due to mean reversion following the sharp Nov 21 drop. As the forecast horizon extends to Dec 23 and Feb 21, underlying economic uncertainty (such as potential Fed rate adjustments and macroeconomic trends) pushes the expected probabilities closer to 50% (0.5), although they remain slightly elevated above 0.5 due to the relatively low 6.044 starting anchor.
